# Server-side repo-clone ingestion exploration (post-MVP cloud deployment)

## Context

Post-MVP residual workstream under Epic #11730. Epic #11720's MVP cloud deployment uses a **push-based** tenant-repo ingestion model (Sub E #11726) — the external dev team pushes source / parsed chunks to the deployed Knowledge Base. Discussion #11718 identified server-side cloning (the deployment pulls/clones the tenant repo itself) as a higher-blast-radius alternative explicitly deferred from the MVP.

## Gating Condition

**Pursued only if #11720's push-based ingestion model (Sub E #11726) proves insufficient in practice** — e.g. the external team needs the deployment to autonomously refresh from their repo without an explicit push step. Until the gate triggers this ticket stays open-but-not-started: captured intent for a future session, not active work.

## Scope

- Evaluate server-side clone / pull-based refresh as an ingestion mode *alongside* (not replacing) push-based.
- The **credentialed-Git-URL contract** for the clone case: URL encoding/validation, credential representation + rejection rules, and no secret persistence in `repoSlug` / logs / manifests / tutorials / graph-visible config.
- Per-`{tenantId, repoSlug}` clone isolation + refresh cadence.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A decision record exists on whether server-side clone is adopted, and if so its trigger vs the push-based default.
- [ ] If adopted: the credentialed-Git-URL contract is specified + implemented with no secret leakage.
- [ ] Push-based ingestion (Sub E #11726) remains the default; clone is strictly additive.

## Out of Scope

- The push-based MVP ingestion model — owned by Sub E #11726.
- Anything on the #11720 MVP critical path.

## Related

- **Parent:** #11730 (Post-MVP Residual Workstreams)
- **Gating input:** #11726 (Sub E — push-based ingestion; this workstream triggers only if that proves insufficient)
- **Origin Discussion:** #11718

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>

* fix(deploy): milestone 5 asserts the MCP tool-error shape of the volume gate (#11725)

The KB volume gate returns a `{error, message, code}` payload; BaseServer's
formatToolResult wraps any `{error}`-bearing result as an isError tool result
(`Tool Error: ... Message: ...`) with no structuredContent. Milestone 5's
readToolJson() call then JSON.parsed that "Tool Error" text and threw a
SyntaxError in CI integration-unified.

Milestone 5 now asserts the path-level refusal directly — result.isError plus
the volume-gate identifiers in the error text — and no longer reaches for the
structured KB_INGEST_VOLUME_EXCEEDED payload, which stays owned in-process by
ai/kb-ingestion/multi-tenant.spec. readToolJson is dropped from the imports.

Co-Authored-By: neo-opus-4-7 <neo-opus-4-7@neomjs.com>
